article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
obligation_text: To the extent the deployer exercises control over the input data, that deployer shall ensure that input data is relevant and sufficiently representative in view of the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.
scoring_text: Skill in AI input data quality management, including relevance assessment, representativeness evaluation for intended purpose, and data quality assurance for deployer-controlled inputs.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI input data quality management, including relevance assessment, representativeness evaluation for intended purpose, and data quality assurance for deployer-controlled inputs.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting organizational data and AI diagnostic assessments, including stakeholder interviews, artifact review, maturity scoring, and finding synthesis.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 26(4) (obligation) superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.564, cosine: 0.602). Secondary: LS-S-008 (LS, cosine: 0.590), LS-S-009 (LS, cosine: 0.590).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.564). Shared vocabulary: [ai, data, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [assessment, assurance, controlled, deployer, evaluation, input] vs WIDAI terms [artifact, assessments, conducting, diagnostic, finding, interviews] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.861, R=0.893). R exceeds P by 0.032 — the WIDAI KSA's 19 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 22 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (90.2%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.602 ≈ STS 0.564 (gap 0.038). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, data, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 18.8%. Shared tokens: [ai, data, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [assessment, assurance, controlled, deployer, evaluation, input]. WIDAI-only: [artifact, assessments, conducting, diagnostic, finding, interviews].
